sources.telos_implication: Report the tested claim, harness, budget, artifacts, and validity checks as part of the result; keep reward-hacking adjustments visible.
sources.title: A shared playbook for trustworthy third party evaluations

sources.telos_implication: Treat LLM judges as scalable graders that need clear rubrics, controls, and validation before scale.
sources.title: Evaluation best practices

sources.telos_implication: Move from isolated outputs to trace-level, repeatable datasets and structured graders when debugging agent workflows.
sources.title: Evaluate agent workflows

sources.telos_implication: Human-reviewed software tasks and careful issue selection remain necessary; Telos should keep execution-backed source traceability explicit.
sources.title: Introducing SWE-bench Verified

sources.telos_implication: Reward hacking on programming tasks is directly relevant to frontier safety, not a cosmetic benchmark issue.
sources.title: From shortcuts to sabotage: natural emergent misalignment from reward hacking

sources.telos_implication: Distribution-specific fixes can hide failures; Telos should test whether a technique generalizes beyond the exact panel prompt.
sources.title: Teaching Claude Why

sources.telos_implication: Goodhart/specification failures should be tested by finding the actual shortcut, not by trusting surface success.
sources.title: Specification gaming: the flip side of AI ingenuity

sources.telos_implication: Evaluation thresholds should be explicit and tied to a decision procedure before a claim changes.
sources.title: Strengthening our Frontier Safety Framework

sources.telos_implication: Provider-native structured outputs should be required for future paid judge or property-generator calls where available.
sources.title: Structured outputs

sources.telos_implication: Agent evaluations need scoring functions and task implementations that resist gaming or subversion by tool-using models.
sources.title: AI models can cheat on evaluations?

sources.telos_implication: Telos should keep multi-metric, transparent evidence rather than collapsing reward-hack verification into one scalar.
sources.title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models

sources.telos_implication: Adaptive selection is acceptable only after the pool and stopping bars are frozen; future Telos expansion should prioritize high-information panel misses.
sources.title: Reliable and Efficient Amortized Model-Based Evaluation

sources.telos_implication: Fair, repeatable agent benchmarks with tool-strategy analysis are active academic work; Telos should expose failure modes, not just aggregate counts.
sources.title: Research Fair Spring 2026 agent benchmark projects

sources.telos_implication: Inference-time reward use needs proxy-risk controls; Telos should not let a reward model or judge become the trusted final oracle.
sources.title: Reward Shaping for (Inference-Time) Alignment

sources.telos_implication: Risk evidence should be taxonomy-linked and useful for auditors; Telos should produce mapped failure modes and mitigations.
sources.title: MIT AI Risk Initiative

sources.telos_implication: Evaluator tampering and leakage should be measured as first-class outcomes; Telos should keep prompt/data leakage and artifact hashes in every run.
sources.title: RewardHackingAgents

sources.telos_implication: Reward-hack opportunities should be made verifiable by design; Telos should turn panel misses into execution-verifiable property probes.
sources.title: Hack-Verifiable Environments

sources.telos_implication: Proxy-reward failures can appear zero-shot in language agents; Telos should measure hidden-objective completion, not just observed reward.
sources.title: Reward Hacking in Language Model Agents

sources.telos_implication: LLM-as-judge needs reliability strategy and meta-evaluation; Telos should escalate missed rows to executable evidence rather than only asking more judges.
sources.title: A Survey on LLM-as-a-Judge

sources.telos_implication: Long-horizon coding-agent evaluation is moving toward harder, contamination-resistant tasks; Telos should keep public score claims out until packaging matures.
sources.title: SWE-Bench Pro
